title: Spec: Simplify addressing to link-local + Yggdrasil only
description: Update all normative specs and supporting documentation to use link-local control addresses plus per-node native Yggdrasil 0200::/8 /128 application addresses. Remove ULA and baseline GUA assignment. Define current upstream AddrForKey exactly, local-only baseline routing, realm-local multicast, SCHC rule-set v2, trust binding, collision handling, and independent vectors.
design: Use current upstream yggdrasil-go AddrForKey compatibility, not the issue's obsolete SHA-512 sketch. Native addresses are 0200::/8 per-node /128s derived directly from the raw Ed25519 public key. Link-local IID remains SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8] with the U/L bit cleared, matching current Python and Zephyr implementations. ULA and baseline GUA are removed. Local full-/128 routes win; unknown native destinations use an operational identity-preserving Yggdrasil handoff or fail unreachable. An ordinary gateway-owned Yggdrasil daemon cannot impersonate node-native sources; 0300::/8 gateway subnets are rejected because they violate stable per-node addressing.
acceptance_criteria: Normative architecture, network, routing, security, SCHC, RPL, LCI, packet examples, and application examples consistently specify link-local control plus native per-node Yggdrasil /128 application addressing; no baseline ULA remains; exact AddrForKey algorithm and an upstream-derived test vector are included; global handoff limitation is explicit; documentation checks and three independent reviews pass.
notes: Reviewed draft checkpoint pending convergence. Remaining blockers: distinct RPL Instance IDs and authenticated sender realm membership; relay algorithm enforcement of origin/immediate-sender/realm aggregate budgets; ambiguous short SenderID must force extended form; scoped link-local examples; stale non-SOS multicast examples; root-DIO key bootstrap; mandatory Announce replay persistence; exact DIO base layout; singleton realm option; RFC 8949 SOS deterministic encoding profile; monotonic trust-anchor generation; explicit OSCORE peer-key/native-source binding; exact group mutation signatures/replay and mandatory rekey after removal; rekey before 24-bit link counter reuse. python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py intentionally fails on the old direct-routing assertion; project-LICHEN-jtup owns runtime/test alignment. Do not begin affected implementation lanes until this issue closes.
